hero.headline: Healthcare Technology, Proven Globally
hero.subtext: 120+ medical device integrations through Hicare Hub. 10,000+ VA gateway exports. RPM/CCM solutions in active use — HicareNet's technology is proven across global healthcare markets.

technology.title: Built on 20+ Years of Medical Device Innovation
technology.description: From medical device innovation to cloud-based care management — our technology stack is proven across global healthcare markets.
technology.hx461_desc: Medical-grade gateway devices with 20+ years of field-proven reliability. The HH-800A (commercialized 2015) integrates essential telehealth functions including patient remote care capabilities. The HH-930 (commercialized 2021) offers enhanced connectivity with 120+ sensor integrations, competitive pricing, and support for modern communication protocols.
technology.hub_desc: IoT gateway supporting 120+ medical devices across USB, Bluetooth Classic, and BLE protocols. Built on IEEE 11073 personal health device standards and Continua Design Guidelines (ITU-T H.810), ensuring plug-and-play interoperability. Deployed in US VA (Veterans Affairs) healthcare projects.
technology.mobile_desc: Mobile health monitoring app connecting patients to their care team. Supports Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) medical device pairing, real-time vital sign tracking, and secure data transmission following ISO/IEEE 11073 health device communication standards.
technology.devices_desc: Comprehensive medical device connectivity across three communication protocols — USB for legacy devices, Bluetooth Classic for continuous data streaming, and BLE for battery-powered wearables. Our platform follows IEEE 11073 standards, similar to global leaders like MedM, supporting 120+ device types including blood pressure monitors, pulse oximeters, glucose meters, weight scales, thermometers, and ECG devices.
technology.story_arc_desc: From InSung Info healthcare division heritage to VA project export (10,000+ gateways) to developing and supplying RPM/CCM solutions for the US market — a proven technology evolution.

fhir.headline: From HL7v2 to FHIR: Our Interoperability Journey
fhir.subtext: Building on proven HL7 Version 2 interoperability testing, we're bringing FHIR R4 standard APIs to connect your EHR with our platform seamlessly.

cta.headline: Ready to Experience Proven Healthcare Technology?
cta.subtext: With 120+ device integrations — discover the technology platform trusted by US VA projects and 40+ hospitals.

hero.headline: Technology-Driven Healthcare Solutions
hero.subtext: Built on 20+ years of healthcare technology expertise and 120+ medical device integrations, we deliver RPM, CCM, AWV, and Value-Based Care solutions proven in global markets.

rpm.title: Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)
rpm.description: Built on the proven Hicare platform with 120+ device integrations and 20+ years of healthcare technology heritage, our RPM solution enables proactive care with continuous vital sign monitoring and automated clinical alerts.
rpm.feature1: 120+ medical device types supported via Hicare Hub IoT gateway
rpm.feature2: Real-time vital sign tracking powered by proven device connectivity
rpm.feature4: Seamless data integration through our MedM-class device platform
rpm.outcome1: Reduce hospital readmissions by up to 38%
rpm.outcome3: Generate new revenue streams with CPT 99453-99458

ccm.title: Chronic Care Management (CCM)
ccm.description: Powered by the Hicare platform's real-time device connectivity and data infrastructure, our CCM solution coordinates care for patients with chronic conditions through multilingual engagement and personalized care plans.
ccm.feature1: Multilingual care coordination (English, Korean, and Spanish)

awv.title: Annual Wellness Visits (AWV)
awv.description: Leveraging the Hicare platform's proven healthcare data infrastructure, our AWV solution streamlines preventive care delivery with automated Health Risk Assessments and Medicare compliance support.
awv.outcome1: Increase AWV completion rates by up to 45%
awv.outcome2: Reduce administrative burden on clinical staff
awv.outcome3: Capture missed preventive care revenue with G0438/G0439

vbc.title: Value-Based Care
vbc.description: Backed by 120+ device integrations and continuous monitoring data from the Hicare platform, our VBC solution helps you succeed in value-based payment models with quality measure dashboards and outcomes-based reporting.
vbc.outcome1: Improve quality scores across HEDIS and Stars measures
vbc.outcome2: Reduce total cost of care per patient
vbc.outcome3: Maximize shared savings and incentive payments

fhir.title: FHIR-Based EMR Integration
fhir.description: Building on our proven HL7 Version 2 interoperability testing experience — the standard used by 95% of US healthcare organizations — we are extending our platform to support HL7 FHIR R4 APIs for modern cloud-based and mobile EHR integrations.
fhir.q1_desc: Building and certifying FHIR R4 endpoints with comprehensive testing and compliance validation.
fhir.q2_desc: Rolling out integration with select partner clinics for real-world validation and feedback.
fhir.h2_desc: Full production release with support for all major EHR systems and seamless data exchange.

implementation.title: Get Started in as Little as 8 Weeks
implementation.description: Our streamlined implementation process gets your practice up and running quickly with full support at every step.
implementation.step1_desc: Assess your practice needs, current workflows, and integration requirements.
implementation.step2_desc: Set up your platform, configure clinical thresholds, and connect devices.
implementation.step3_desc: Comprehensive staff training on platform usage, workflows, and best practices.
implementation.step4_desc: Launch with dedicated support, monitor performance, and optimize outcomes.

cta.headline: Ready to Get Started?
cta.subtext: Let us show you how HicareNet's proven technology — from 20+ years of healthcare expertise to our 120+ device integration platform — can transform your practice.

hero.headline: About HicareNet
hero.subtext: From InSung Info healthcare division heritage to US VA deployment — a healthcare technology company with a proven track record of global innovation.

story.title: From Device Maker to Global Healthcare Platform
story.p1: HicareNet was established in 2020 as an independent corporation through physical division from InSung Info's healthcare business division. Building on 20+ years of accumulated remote patient monitoring technology, HicareNet aims to commercialize digital healthcare platforms globally.
story.p2: The company's technology evolved into the Hicare Hub gateway, capable of connecting 120+ FDA-approved medical device types. The Hicare Hub was exported to the US VA (Veterans Affairs) projects with 10,000+ units, proving our technology internationally. Additional exports include 1,000 units to Italy and 3,000 units to France.
story.p3: Today, HicareNet applies this proven technology to develop and supply RPM and CCM solutions. We serve 40+ hospitals across California, with 3,000+ enrolled patients, combining 20+ years of healthcare technology heritage with modern cloud-based care management.
story.milestone1_desc: Built on 20+ years of healthcare IT expertise from InSung Info's healthcare division. Developed deep capabilities in patient vital sign monitoring, chronic disease management, and non-face-to-face diagnosis/monitoring technology.
story.milestone2_desc: In 2020, HicareNet was established as an independent corporation through physical division from InSung Info's healthcare business division. The goal: global commercialization of the digital healthcare platform built on 20+ years of accumulated technology.
story.milestone3_desc: In 2021, HicareNet established its US subsidiary. The Hicare Hub gateway, supporting 120+ medical devices, was deployed in US VA (Veterans Affairs) healthcare projects with 10,000+ units exported. Additional gateway exports: 1,000 units to Italy, 3,000 units to France.
story.milestone4_desc: RPM/CCM service launched in April 2023, starting with 15 hospitals and 1,000 patients in California. By 2024, expanded to 37 hospitals and 2,000+ patients. In 2025, serving 40+ hospitals with 3,000+ enrolled patients and 300+ monthly insurance claims.

hero.headline: Security & Compliance
hero.subtext: HIPAA compliant and ISO certified — HicareNet's commitment to security and regulatory compliance is built into our DNA.

heritage.name: 20+ Years of Healthcare IT
heritage.title: Proven Technology Foundation
heritage.description: HicareNet builds on 20+ years of healthcare IT expertise inherited from InSung Info's healthcare division. Our technology heritage spans remote patient monitoring, chronic disease management, and medical device connectivity — proven across global markets including the US, Italy, and France.
heritage.feature1: 120+ FDA-approved medical device integrations via Bluetooth auto-pairing
heritage.feature2: 10,000+ Hicare Hub gateways exported to US VA (Veterans Affairs) projects

hipaa.feature2: Role-based access controls with multi-factor authentication

hipaa.feature5: Business Associate Agreements (BAA) with all partners

security.title: How We Protect Your Data
security.description: Our multi-layered security approach ensures your data is protected at every level.
security.encryption_desc: AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit for all data
security.access_desc: Role-based permissions with MFA and principle of least privilege
security.monitoring_desc: 24/7 security monitoring with automated threat detection and response
security.backup_desc: Geo-redundant backups with 99.9% uptime SLA and rapid recovery procedures
security.infra_desc: Hosted on HIPAA-eligible cloud infrastructure with SOC 2 Type II compliance. Physically secure data centers with 99.99% uptime SLA.
security.incident_desc: Documented incident response plan with defined escalation paths. 24-hour breach notification commitment and regular tabletop exercises.

q1.question: Do you sign Business Associate Agreements (BAA)?
q1.answer: Yes. We execute BAAs with all covered entities and business associates before any PHI is shared. Our standard BAA covers all services and can be customized to meet your organization's requirements.

q2.question: How often are your security certifications audited?
q2.answer: Our ISO 27001 and ISO 27701 certifications undergo annual surveillance audits and full recertification every three years by an accredited third-party auditor. HIPAA compliance assessments are conducted annually.

q3.question: Where is patient data stored?
q3.answer: All patient data is stored in HIPAA-compliant, SOC 2 Type II certified data centers located within the United States. We use geo-redundant storage to ensure data availability and disaster recovery.

q4.question: How does HicareNet handle data breach notification?
q4.answer: We maintain a comprehensive incident response plan that includes immediate containment, forensic investigation, and notification procedures. In compliance with HIPAA and state regulations, we notify affected covered entities within 24 hours of discovering a confirmed breach, and provide full support throughout the remediation process.

cta.headline: Have Compliance Questions?
cta.subtext: Our security team is available to discuss your compliance requirements and answer any questions about our certifications.

q1.question: What services does HicareNet provide?
q1.answer: HicareNet provides comprehensive healthcare technology solutions built on 20+ years of healthcare technology heritage. Our offerings include Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM), Chronic Care Management (CCM), Annual Wellness Visits (AWV), and Value-Based Care support. Powered by 20+ years of medical device expertise, Hicare Hub IoT gateway with 120+ device integrations, and Hicare M mobile monitoring, our platform delivers multilingual care coordination in English, Korean, and Spanish, automated Health Risk Assessments, and quality measure tracking.

q2.question: Which types of healthcare practices do you work with?
q2.answer: We work with a wide range of healthcare providers including primary care clinics, specialty practices, Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs), Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs), Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs), and health systems of all sizes. Our solutions are designed to scale from solo practices to large multi-site organizations.

q3.question: How long does it take to implement your solutions?
q3.answer: Our streamlined onboarding process typically takes 8 weeks from initial discovery to go-live. This includes practice assessment, platform configuration, staff training, and dedicated launch support. Complex multi-site implementations may require additional time, which we plan during the discovery phase.

q1.question: What CPT codes can I bill with HicareNet services?
q1.answer: Our RPM program supports CPT codes 99453, 99454, 99457, and 99458. CCM services use CPT codes 99490, 99491, 99487, and 99489. Annual Wellness Visits are billed under G0438 (initial) and G0439 (subsequent). Our billing support team helps ensure you capture all eligible reimbursement.

q2.question: Do you provide billing and coding support?
q2.answer: Yes. Our team includes certified medical coders who provide documentation guidance, coding support, and claims assistance. We also offer real-time billing dashboards so you can track reimbursement across all programs and identify revenue optimization opportunities.

q1.question: What monitoring devices do you support?
q1.answer: Through our Hicare Hub IoT gateway, we support 120+ medical device types including blood pressure monitors, pulse oximeters, glucose meters, weight scales, thermometers, and more. Our device connectivity platform — built on 20+ years of medical device expertise — uses cellular or Bluetooth connectivity for automatic data transmission. We ship devices directly to patients with simple setup instructions in their preferred language.

q2.question: Can your platform integrate with our EHR system?
q2.answer: We currently offer integration capabilities with major EHR systems through standard interfaces. Our upcoming FHIR R4 integration (launching H2 2026) will provide seamless bidirectional data exchange with all ONC-certified EHR systems, enabling real-time clinical data synchronization.

1.quote: HicareNet's RPM solution has transformed how we manage our chronic disease patients. Hospital readmissions dropped 35% in the first year, and our patients feel more connected to their care team.

2.quote: The multilingual care coordination is a game-changer for our diverse patient population. Our CCM enrollment increased by 60% once patients could communicate in their preferred language.

3.quote: We went from completing 40 AWVs per month to over 120 with HicareNet's automated HRA system. The efficiency gains have been remarkable, and our Medicare wellness revenue doubled.

hero.headline: Privacy Policy
hero.subtext: Your privacy and the protection of your health information are fundamental to our mission.

collect.title: 1. Information We Collect
collect.intro: HicareNet, Inc. ("HicareNet," "we," "us," or "our"), a US subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd., collects information necessary to deliver our healthcare technology services. The types of information we collect include:
collect.personal1: Name, email address, phone number, and mailing address
collect.personal3: Account login credentials and authentication data
collect.health1: Patient demographic information as required for Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) services
collect.health2: Clinical data including vital signs, biometric readings, and health assessments
collect.health3: Annual Wellness Visit (AWV) assessment data and care plan information
collect.health4: Treatment records and care coordination communications
collect.usage1: Device information, browser type, and operating system
collect.usage2: IP address and approximate geographic location
collect.usage3: Platform usage patterns, feature interactions, and session duration

use.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
use.intro: We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
use.delivery1: Providing and maintaining our RPM, CCM, AWV, and Value-Based Care platform services
use.delivery2: Processing and transmitting clinical data between healthcare providers and patients
use.delivery3: Generating care plans, clinical reports, and compliance documentation
use.delivery4: Facilitating FHIR-based EMR integrations and health information exchange
use.improvement1: Analyzing aggregated, de-identified data to improve platform performance and reliability
use.improvement2: Developing new features and enhancing existing clinical workflows
use.improvement3: Conducting quality assurance testing and security audits
use.communication1: Sending service-related notifications, system alerts, and account updates
use.communication2: Responding to support requests and inquiries
use.communication3: Providing regulatory updates and compliance notifications relevant to your organization

hipaa.title: 3. HIPAA Compliance
hipaa.intro: As a healthcare technology provider, HicareNet operates in full compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) and the HITECH Act.
hipaa.phiDesc: All Protected Health Information processed through our platform is handled in accordance with HIPAA Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules. We implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of PHI.
hipaa.baaDesc: HicareNet enters into Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) with all covered entities and their business associates before accessing or processing any PHI. Our BAA is based on the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services model agreement and is reviewed annually.
hipaa.minimumDesc: We adhere to the HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard, limiting the use and disclosure of PHI to the minimum amount reasonably necessary to accomplish the intended purpose. Role-based access controls enforce this principle across all systems and personnel.

sharing.title: 4. Data Sharing and Disclosure
sharing.intro: We do not sell your personal information or PHI. We may share information in the following circumstances:
sharing.item1: Healthcare Providers: With authorized healthcare providers and care team members as necessary for treatment, payment, and healthcare operations
sharing.item2: Service Providers: With vetted third-party service providers who are bound by BAAs and confidentiality obligations and who assist in operating our platform
sharing.item3: Legal Requirements: When required by applicable law, court order, subpoena, or government regulation
sharing.item4: Business Transfers: In connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with appropriate privacy protections maintained
sharing.item5: With Consent: With your explicit written authorization for any purpose not described in this policy

security.title: 5. Data Security
security.intro: HicareNet implements comprehensive security measures to protect your information, backed by internationally recognized certifications:
security.item1: Encryption: AES-256 encryption for data at rest and TLS 1.3 for data in transit, with regularly rotated encryption keys
security.item2: Access Controls: Role-based access control (RBAC) with mandatory multi-factor authentication for all system access
security.item3: ISO 27001 Certification: Our Information Security Management System is independently audited and certified to ISO 27001 standards
security.item4: ISO 27701 Certification: Our Privacy Information Management System extends ISO 27001 with specific privacy controls for PII protection
security.item5: Monitoring: 24/7 security monitoring with intrusion detection systems, SIEM integration, and automated alerting
security.item6: Incident Response: Documented incident response plan with 24-hour breach notification commitment to affected parties

rights.title: 6. Your Rights
rights.intro: Depending on your jurisdiction and applicable law (including HIPAA and the California Consumer Privacy Act), you may have the following rights regarding your information:
rights.item1: Access: Request a copy of the personal information and PHI we hold about you
rights.item2: Correction: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal information
rights.item3: Deletion: Request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal retention requirements and exceptions
rights.item4: Data Portability: Receive your data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format
rights.item5: Restriction: Request restriction of processing of your personal information under certain circumstances
rights.item6: Accounting of Disclosures: Request an accounting of certain disclosures of your PHI as provided under HIPAA
rights.exercise: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us using the information provided in the Contact Information section below. We will respond to verified requests within 30 days, or as required by applicable law.

cookies.title: 7. Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: Our website and platform use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your experience and collect usage data.
cookies.type1: Essential Cookies: Required for core platform functionality, authentication, and security. These cannot be disabled.
cookies.type2: Performance Cookies: Collect anonymized data about how visitors use our website, helping us improve site performance and user experience.
cookies.type3: Functional Cookies: Remember your preferences such as language selection and display settings.
cookies.manageDesc: You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings. Please note that disabling essential cookies may affect platform functionality. We do not use advertising or tracking cookies.

retention.title: 8. Data Retention
retention.desc: We retain personal information and PHI for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements. Healthcare records are retained in accordance with applicable federal and state retention requirements. When data is no longer required, it is securely destroyed using methods appropriate to the sensitivity of the information.

changes.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
changes.desc: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically to reflect changes in our practices, technologies, legal requirements, or other factors. When we make material changes, we will notify you by posting the updated policy on our website with a revised "Last Updated" date and, where required, provide additional notice through email or in-platform notification. We encourage you to review this policy regularly.

contact.title: 10. Contact Information
contact.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, wish to exercise your rights, or have concerns about our data practices, please contact us:

hero.headline: Terms of Service
hero.subtext: Please read these terms carefully before using our healthcare technology platform.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.desc1: By accessing or using the HicareNet platform, website, and related services (collectively, the "Services") provided by HicareNet, Inc. ("HicareNet," "we," "us," or "our"), a US subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd., you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Services.
acceptance.desc2: If you are accessing or using the Services on behalf of a healthcare organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms, and "you" will refer to both you individually and the organization.

services.title: 2. Description of Services
services.intro: HicareNet provides a comprehensive healthcare technology platform that includes the following services:
services.item1: Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM): Technology-enabled collection and transmission of patient health data from connected devices, with clinical monitoring and alert management
services.item2: Chronic Care Management (CCM): Care coordination services and tools for managing patients with two or more chronic conditions, including care plan development and patient engagement
services.item3: Annual Wellness Visits (AWV): Digital assessment tools and workflow automation for Medicare Annual Wellness Visits, including health risk assessments and personalized prevention plans
services.item4: Value-Based Care (VBC): Analytics, reporting, and population health management tools to support value-based care models and quality measure optimization
services.item5: FHIR Integration: Interoperability services enabling health data exchange through HL7 FHIR standards for seamless EMR connectivity (available H1 2026)
services.disclaimer: The Services are designed to support healthcare providers in delivering care and are not intended to replace professional medical judgment. All clinical decisions remain the responsibility of licensed healthcare professionals.

accounts.title: 3. User Accounts and Responsibilities
accounts.intro: To access the Services, you must create an account and provide accurate, complete, and current information. You are responsible for:
accounts.item1: Maintaining the confidentiality and security of your account credentials, including passwords and multi-factor authentication tokens
accounts.item2: All activities that occur under your account, whether authorized by you or not
accounts.item3: Promptly notifying HicareNet of any unauthorized use of your account or any other security breach
accounts.item4: Ensuring that all users within your organization are properly trained on platform usage and data handling protocols
accounts.item5: Maintaining appropriate professional licenses and credentials required to use clinical features of the Services

acceptable.title: 4. Acceptable Use Policy
acceptable.intro: You agree not to use the Services to:
acceptable.item1: Violate any applicable federal, state, or local law or regulation, including HIPAA, HITECH, and state privacy laws
acceptable.item2: Access, use, or disclose Protected Health Information (PHI) in any manner not permitted by HIPAA or your applicable Business Associate Agreement
acceptable.item3: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any systems, networks, or data, or interfere with the security of the Services
acceptable.item4: Transmit malicious software, viruses, or any code designed to disrupt, damage, or limit the functionality of the Services
acceptable.item5: Reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code of the Services
acceptable.item6: Share account credentials or allow unauthorized individuals to access the Services through your account
acceptable.item7: Use the Services for any purpose other than legitimate healthcare operations as defined under HIPAA

ip.title: 5. Intellectual Property
ip.desc1: The Services, including all software, algorithms, user interfaces, designs, documentation, and related intellectual property, are owned by HicareNet, Inc. and Hicare Co., Ltd. and are protected by United States and international copyright, trademark, patent, and trade secret laws.
ip.desc2: Subject to these Terms, we grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Services solely for your internal healthcare operations. This license does not include the right to sublicense, modify, adapt, translate, or create derivative works based on the Services.
ip.desc3: You retain ownership of all data you input into the Services. By using the Services, you grant HicareNet a limited license to process, store, and transmit your data solely as necessary to provide the Services and as permitted by applicable law and your Business Associate Agreement.

hipaa.title: 6. HIPAA and Healthcare Compliance
hipaa.desc1: HicareNet operates as a Business Associate under HIPAA when processing PHI on behalf of Covered Entities. Our obligations regarding PHI are governed by the Business Associate Agreement ("BAA") executed between HicareNet and each Covered Entity.
hipaa.item1: A valid BAA must be in place before any PHI is transmitted through the Services
hipaa.item2: You are responsible for obtaining all necessary patient consents and authorizations as required by HIPAA and applicable state laws
hipaa.item3: You will use the Services only for permitted purposes under HIPAA, including treatment, payment, and healthcare operations
hipaa.item4: You will promptly report to HicareNet any suspected or confirmed security incidents involving PHI processed through the Services
hipaa.item5: HicareNet maintains ISO 27001 and ISO 27701 certifications and implements security controls consistent with the HIPAA Security Rule

liability.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
liability.desc1: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL HICARENET, ITS AFFILIATES, DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, OR AGENTS BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, USE, OR GOODWILL, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THESE TERMS OR THE USE OF THE SERVICES.
liability.desc2: HICARENET'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID BY YOU TO HICARENET DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM.
liability.desc3: Nothing in these Terms shall limit liability for gross negligence, willful misconduct, fraud, or any liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law, including obligations under HIPAA.

disclaimers.title: 8. Disclaimers
disclaimers.desc1: THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
disclaimers.desc2: HicareNet does not warrant that the Services will be uninterrupted, error-free, or completely secure. While we implement industry-standard security measures, no system can guarantee absolute security against all threats.
disclaimers.desc3: The Services do not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. The Services are tools designed to assist licensed healthcare professionals in their clinical practice. HicareNet is not responsible for clinical decisions made using data processed through the Services.

indemnification.title: 9. Indemnification
indemnification.desc: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless HicareNet, its affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to: (a) your use of the Services; (b) your violation of these Terms; (c) your violation of any applicable law or regulation; or (d) your negligent or wrongful acts or omissions in connection with your use of the Services.

termination.title: 10. Termination
termination.desc1: Either party may terminate these Terms and your access to the Services upon thirty (30) days written notice. HicareNet may suspend or terminate your access immediately if you breach these Terms, engage in prohibited conduct, or pose a security risk to the platform or other users.
termination.desc2: Upon termination, we will provide a reasonable period (not less than 30 days) for you to export your data from the Services. After this period, we may delete your data in accordance with our data retention policy, subject to any legal retention requirements. Provisions regarding intellectual property, limitation of liability, indemnification, and governing law survive termination.

governing.title: 11. Governing Law
governing.desc1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to these Terms shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in the State of California.
governing.desc2: To the extent that any provision of these Terms conflicts with applicable federal healthcare regulations, including HIPAA, the federal regulations shall take precedence. Nothing in these Terms is intended to modify or waive any rights or obligations under HIPAA or the HITECH Act.

changes.title: 12. Changes to Terms
changes.desc: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated at least thirty (30) days prior to taking effect through email notification and prominent notice on our website. Your continued use of the Services after the effective date of any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the modified Terms, you must discontinue use of the Services before the changes take effect.

contact.title: 13. Contact Information
contact.intro: If you have questions about these Terms of Service or need to provide legal notices, please contact us:
